steps.title: Trace the borrowing path
steps.content: Both examples show Indigenous words reaching English through Spanish. Cacao came from Nahuatl into Spanish and then English, and iwana came from Taíno into Spanish before entering English as "iguana." The question asks what this pattern shows about Spanish specifically.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: B invents a comparison between Spanish and Indigenous contributions. C reverses the direction by claiming Spanish contributed words to Indigenous languages. D invents a numerical comparison between Nahuatl and Taíno borrowings. The answer is A.
